summary: Top Banners (auth-fail + ext-orphaned) migrated onto one token system. (1) ext-orphaned banner inline cssText: raw hex (#2a1d10/#ffd84d/#7a7672/#9b9892/#0a0a0b), var(--bb-amber), and the rgba feedback fills route to GAM_TOK; magic z-index 99999999 -> --z-toast. (2) auth-fail banner: __authBannerSeverity opaque .95 rgba bgs become severity-soft tints + solid severity borders mapped to GAM_TOK (info/warn/danger); z-index 2147483640 -> --z-toast; #fff / #a02020 / rgba(255,255,255,.x) button + container literals -> GAM_TOK; title cssText hex-free. (3) The AUTHORITATIVE auth-fail CSS block (26045-26076), which currently overrides the inline styles via !important and pulls from the parallel --bb-* system, is rewritten onto var(--gam-tok-*): severity-aware [data-severity] backgrounds (severity-soft + colored 3px left keyline + 1px severity border), title color reconciled to the token severity, and one shared button treatment with explicit hover/focus-visible + a disabled-state visual (auth-fail's btnRetry sets disabled with no feedback today). Net: both banners token-driven, no raw rgba/hex outside GAM_TOK, no dual style system, z-literals on the ladder. All locked selectors restyled in place, never renamed; no accessible-name textContent changed.

edits.oldString: b.style.cssText = 'position:fixed;top:0;left:0;right:0;z-index:99999999;background:#2a1d10;border-bottom:1px solid var(--bb-amber);color:#ffd84d;font:600 12px/1.4 ui-monospace,JetBrains Mono,monospace;padding:8px 14px;display:flex;align-items:center;gap:12px;letter-spacing:0.04em;box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.6)';
edits.note: Ext-orphaned shell: magic z-index 99999999 -> --z-toast ladder tier (inline can reference the CSS var here because it is a numeric z-index, not a color; safe per the brief's z-mapping). Hardcoded #2a1d10 warm-brown bg + var(--bb-amber) border + #ffd84d text collapse onto the warn token family (warnSoft bg + warn border/keyline + warn text) = severity-soft notification shell consistent with WP-03. rgba(0,0,0,0.6) shadow -> GAM_TOK.scrim. Kills the --bb-amber dependency + all raw hex on this line.

edits.oldString: '<button id="gam-ext-orphaned-copy" style="background:transparent;border:1px solid #7a7672;color:#ffd84d;padding:4px 10px;cursor:pointer;font:600 10px ui-monospace,monospace;letter-spacing:0.06em;text-transform:uppercase" title="Copy debug details to clipboard">📋 Copy</button>' +
edits.newString: '<button id="gam-ext-orphaned-copy" class="gam-toast-btn" style="background:transparent;border:1px solid '+GAM_TOK.borderStrong+';color:'+GAM_TOK.warn+';padding:4px 10px;cursor:pointer;font:600 10px ui-monospace,monospace;letter-spacing:0.06em;text-transform:uppercase" title="Copy debug details to clipboard">📋 Copy</button>' +
edits.note: Copy button: #7a7672 ghost border -> borderStrong, #ffd84d text -> warn token (matches banner family). Adds shared .gam-toast-btn class for the one button treatment (hover/focus/disabled handled in CSS). Locked id #gam-ext-orphaned-copy + textContent untouched.

Primary reload button: var(--bb-amber) fill -> GAM_TOK.accent (single amber), #0a0a0b text -> onAccentDark (WCAG black-on-amber 9.86). Shared .gam-toast-btn + --primary modifier. Locked id + 'Reload page' accessible name preserved.

edits.oldString: '<button id="gam-ext-orphaned-dismiss" style="background:transparent;border:1px solid #7a7672;color:#9b9892;padding:4px 10px;cursor:pointer;font:600 10px ui-monospace,monospace;letter-spacing:0.06em;text-transform:uppercase">Dismiss</button>'; /* v.next a11y: border 2.5:1 -> 4.1:1 */
edits.newString: '<button id="gam-ext-orphaned-dismiss" class="gam-toast-btn" style="background:transparent;border:1px solid '+GAM_TOK.borderStrong+';color:'+GAM_TOK.inkMuted+';padding:4px 10px;cursor:pointer;font:600 10px ui-monospace,monospace;letter-spacing:0.06em;text-transform:uppercase">Dismiss</button>'; /* v.next a11y: border 2.5:1 -> 4.1:1 */
edits.note: Dismiss button: #7a7672 border -> borderStrong, #9b9892 (legacy ink-muted) -> GAM_TOK.inkMuted (WCAG-bumped #b0b5bc wins per brief 2.2). Shared .gam-toast-btn class. Locked id + 'Dismiss' name preserved.
Copy-feedback fills: raw success/danger rgba -> successSoft/dangerSoft tokens (the .18 alpha collapses to the canonical .12 soft tokens; visually equivalent tint, removes raw rgba).

edits.newString: try { copy.textContent = orig; copy.style.background = 'transparent'; } catch (_) {} /* WP-13: revert to transparent; styled bg handled by .gam-toast-btn */
edits.note: No color literal here (transparent is a keyword, not hex); annotated only to document the toast-btn revert path. Kept verbatim aside from trailing comment so the array stays self-consistent and the oldString is unambiguous.

Auth-fail SETUP tier (no_token/short_token): opaque blue .95 fill -> infoSoft tint + solid info border. Authoritative bg/border come from the CSS [data-severity='setup'] rule below; inline kept hex-free as the var()-unsafe fallback.
Auth-fail CONNECTIVITY tier (fetch_failed/no_response): yellow .95 -> warnSoft tint + warn border. Routes onto the single warn token (the brief's caution/watch language).

Auth-fail CREDENTIAL tier (whoami_status/whoami_empty): amber .95 -> warnSoft + warn. Both connectivity and credential are caution-class; they remain distinguishable by kind/title + the per-severity CSS keyline, not by a bespoke rgba.

Auth-fail UNKNOWN/catch-all tier: opaque red .95 -> dangerSoft tint + solid danger border (#f04040 WCAG-bumped token wins over the old #dc2828). 6-space indent (return object, not nested in an if) keeps this oldString distinct from the indented tiers above.

Auth-fail shell: magic z-index 2147483640 -> --z-toast tier; #fff body text -> GAM_TOK.ink (CSS rule still wins via !important, but inline is now hex-free); rgba(0,0,0,.5) shadow -> GAM_TOK.scrim. _sev.bg/_sev.borderColor now carry tokens from __authBannerSeverity edits above.

Title inline gets an explicit ink color so the inline layer is self-consistent; the authoritative per-severity title color is set by the CSS [data-severity] > div:first-child rules (reconciles the prior CSS-var(--bb-red) vs inline mismatch called out in transformation #4).

Force re-hydrate button: translucent-white border/bg -> borderStrong + surfaceOverlay well, #fff text -> ink. Adds .gam-toast-btn so it inherits the shared hover/focus-visible/disabled treatment (this is the button that sets disabled with zero feedback today).
Open ModTools popup (primary CTA): white fill + #a02020 maroon text -> accent fill + onAccentDark text (single amber, WCAG-clean). .gam-toast-btn--primary modifier for the shared primary treatment.
Dismiss button: translucent-white border -> border token, #fff @ .85 opacity -> inkMuted (proper muted text instead of opacity hack). .gam-toast-btn shared class.

Authoritative auth-fail CSS rewritten onto var(--gam-tok-*): kills the parallel --bb-panel/--bb-red/--bb-red-bg/--bb-ink/--bb-bg/--bb-line-hot/--bb-amber/--bb-amber-bg dependency (the dual style system). Adds [data-severity] severity-soft backgrounds + 3px colored left keyline + matching border + reconciled title color (transformation #1,#2,#4). Adds the missing button :focus-visible AND :disabled visual (transformation #3 — btnRetry sets disabled with zero feedback today). --bb-r (radius), --bb-t-* (type) and --bb-font are layout/type tokens, not colors, and are out of WP-13's color-migration scope, so they stay. rgba(0,0,0,0.6) shadow + the red ring-shadow collapse into the single scrim shadow. The locked selectors #gam-auth-fail-banner and '#gam-auth-fail-banner button' are restyled in place, never renamed; data-severity attr (set by JS at b.dataset.severity) is now wired to visible CSS variants.
